finding: Every init() early-return and the terminal Ready set a status; no exit leaves INIT_STATUS unset, so NotInitialized only applies pre-boot.
Handler match correct: Some/Some->Ready, Some/None->PoolMissing, None->init_status(); PoolMissing hardcoded is accurate for the state-without-pool edge.
host_arch()/host_package() mirror status() inline computation; build_degraded zeroes DB/state fields so degraded and initialized agree on host fields.
available_only swallows list_releases errors into empty Vec like status(); no unwrap/panic; Some/Some still maps status() errors.
Disabled LIST now makes a live GitHub list_releases() per request (previously 503 no-network); matches enabled behavior but new for disabled case; admin-gated + infrequent.
INIT_STATUS first-write-wins correct: init() runs once/process, each call reaches exactly one set_init_status; per-subprocess OnceCell keeps test isolation.
Pre-init read would see NotInitialized default, but module init completes before routes serve; not reachable in production.
VersionStatus derives Serialize/JsonSchema only (no Deserialize), so adding required availability is wire-backward-safe.
Auth gate preserved: RequirePermissions extractor runs before body; only initialized-but-unavailable flips 503->200; install/set-pin/delete keep 503.
New required availability field requires types.ts regen or emit_ts parity fails (done in this change).

The list handler still takes RequirePermissions<(CodeSandboxEnvironmentsRead,)> which runs before the body, so 401/403 is returned before the 200; the degrade path is unreachable without the read perm.
The availability enum exposes only coarse operational states to admins with environments::read; no secrets/paths/credentials leak.
Overlay grants SYS_ADMIN + apparmor/seccomp unconfined + /dev/fuse but correctly avoids --privileged, is opt-in, and not folded into base compose.
envsubst whitelist adds only the boolean ZIEE_CODE_SANDBOX_ENABLED (no secret); value unvalidated but operator-controlled, not an external attack boundary.
Mutating install/set_pin/delete still require Manage + live_pool() 503 when uninitialized; only the read-gated list degrades.
available_only() degrade path is after the Read-perm extractor; unreachable without CodeSandboxEnvironmentsRead.
availability defaults to 'ready', set from all three success paths; degraded 200 never sets error; real load failures still route to error.

Only AvailableRootfsCard gets installDisabled; DownloadedRootfsCard Set-default/Delete stay enabled when degraded (theoretical: degraded response always has installed:[] so downloadedGroups is empty and no buttons render).
Degraded notice uses Alert tone=warning -> role=alert, AA-contrast token, decorative icon; accessible + non-destructive.
Disabled Download button in a Tooltip may not fire hover; mirrors pre-existing requires-manage wrap; the degraded Alert already states the reason prominently.

DEGRADED_NOTICE const declared between import statements (after DownloadedRootfsCard import, before _rootfsShared import); npm run check passed but it violates import-first ordering; move below all imports.
availability as Exclude cast sound (guarded by isDegraded); ?? fallback defensive; testid + Alert-not-ErrorState match conventions.
New seeded surface holdPatches without await whenTrue guard; acceptable (renders from availability, holdPatch out-holds mount-time loadStatus); gallery checks pass.
DEGRADED_NOTICE covers all six non-ready variants (Record<Exclude<...,'ready'>> enforces exhaustiveness); typo-free; each points to the catalog.

New disabled-list tests reach live GitHub via available_only()->list_releases() (no seam); non-hermetic, ~slow on air-gapped; assertions still pass offline (is_array + availability from INIT_STATUS need no network).
Module doc claimed process-global STATE makes 200-vs-503 order-dependent, contradicting the new deterministic 200 asserts; updated doc to note per-subprocess STATE/INIT_STATUS and the LIST degrade.

versionStatus() fixture correctly gained availability:'ready'; new degrade test's mock matches VersionStatus shape incl availability + host fields; exercises degraded-alert + error-absent + install-disabled.
available_only() calls uncached list_releases() per disabled/degraded LIST request; same as the enabled status() path (needed to show the catalog); admin-gated + infrequent; accepted.
Degrade branch structure is clean, no inefficiency beyond the intentional GitHub fetch.
ZIEE_CODE_SANDBOX_ENABLED wired in all three places mirroring ZIEE_UPDATE_CHECK; no literal placeholder can leak into rendered YAML.
Overlay keeps name: ziee-web and overrides the ziee-web service (merges, not forks); base filename correct.
SYS_ADMIN + unconfined + /dev/fuse is broad but the documented least-privilege alternative to --privileged, opt-in with a SECURITY note; acceptable.

installVersion POST reply unconditionally overwrote installTasks[key] (phase null), clobbering SSE-applied progress so a long download appeared stuck on 'queued'; fixed via reconcileInitialTask (existing ?? initial, SSE-authoritative).
reconcileInitialTask returns existing ?? initial — seeds when absent, never downgrades an SSE-advanced or terminal task; pure + unit-tested.
3 node:test cases cover seed-when-absent, no-downgrade-of-downloading, and no-resurrect-terminal — the anti-clobber invariant; wire-accurate fixtures.
